action.reasoning: Observation: The current best is 83.11% from iteration 10. Recent AdamW-based linear-head attempts (iters 14-21) have plateaued around 78-82% due to subtle training-loop instabilities. Analysis: Since the frozen IN21k ViT-B/16 backbone produces excellent features, I can bypass head-optimization entirely by using a non-parametric k-NN classifier in feature space. With all ~45k training features stored in the checkpoint, k-NN with cosine similarity should be robust, suffer zero catastrophic forgetting, and might exceed the linear-head score. It also eliminates learning-rate scheduling bugs. Decision: Implement a pure feature-extraction + k-NN pipeline. train.py will only extract and store all task features (no head training). predict.py will extract test features (with horizontal-flip TTA), then perform weighted k-NN (k=10) by cosine similarity. Run training (~3 min), verify predict.py offline on val.pt, and submit eval. If successful, I can tune k and distance metrics in later iterations.
action.command: python train.py && mkdir -p dummy_test && cp /data/val.pt dummy_test/test.pt && python predict.py --data_path dummy_test --checkpoint checkpoints/model.pt --output dummy_out.json && python -c "import json; d=json.load(open('dummy_out.json')); print('num preds:', len(d['predictions']))"
